dod.title: Definition of Done
dod.description: What must be true for the release-gate to call this run finished.
dod.empty: No acceptance criteria yet — without these the release-gate has nothing to verify.

errors.title: Something went wrong
errors.retryHint: Try again or reload the page.

workers.title: Workers
workers.disabledTooltip: Worker is disabled in server config
workers.emptyDescription: Background jobs (cron) are registered server-side. Once enabled they show up here.

skills.title: Skills
skills.emptyDescription: Add SKILL.md files under .claude/skills or install a plugin that ships skills.
skills.noMatchesDescription: No skills match the current filter or search.

promptBundles.title: Prompt-Bundle Cache
promptBundles.explanation: Each row is a stable working directory + Claude session for a unique (system-prompt, tools, model) combination. Reusing them keeps Anthropic's server-side prompt cache warm.

confirmInvalidateAll.title: Invalidate all bundles?
confirmInvalidateAll.description_one: This drops the cached bundle. The next session re-warms the cache from scratch.
confirmInvalidateAll.description_other: This drops all {{count}} cached bundles. The next session re-warms the cache from scratch.

promptBundles.emptyDescription: Invoke a skill or send a chat message to populate the cache.

insights.title: Insights

empty.title: No trajectories captured yet
empty.description: Each completed run records a trajectory with the prompt, outcome, and lessons learned. They appear here as runs finish.

empty.title: No run summaries yet
empty.description: Once runs complete, a short Markdown summary is generated and appears here.

insights.priorsEmpty: No priors yet — they accrue once Thompson sampling has run.

empty.title: No router priors yet
empty.description: Priors accrue once Thompson sampling has run across enough role/model combinations.

goap.title: Goal Planner
goap.subtitleLong: Define start and goal state, pick available actions — the planner returns the shortest sequence. A standalone sandbox for exploring GOAP; it does not drive your runs.

canvas.overflowTitle: The canvas shows at most 8 nodes — the full plan is listed below.

library.empty: No actions defined. Edit JSON to add some.

errors.noActionsEnabled: No actions enabled — pick at least one action from the library.
errors.malformedAction: Each action needs a string name and a numeric cost.
errors.notBoolean: State values must be true or false.
errors.searchExhausted: Search space too large — reduce the actions or simplify the goal.

result.title: Plan
result.noPlan: No plan exists for this initial/goal/actions combination.
result.malformed: Planner returned an unexpected response — please retry or check the server logs.

toolMultiSelect.customHelp: Add any tool name not in the catalog (e.g. {{example}}).

notes.haiku: Fast and cheap. Good for QA gates, simple file edits, and prompt probes.
notes.opus: Highest quality. Use for architecture and complex reasoning.

agents.title: Agents
agents.subtitleLead: The built-in dev team is always here for chat. Add your own agents for one-off specialties or project-specific personas.

marcus.description: Project Manager · coordinates the team, brainstorms with you, and can create + start projects.

sarah.description: Requirements Interviewer · extracts a complete project brief before planning starts.

riley.description: Native packaging engineer · Tauri / Electron / pkg artifacts.

theo.description: Team Lead · synthesises state + events + handoffs into routing decisions.

elena.description: ML / AI Engineer · LLM pipelines, embeddings, evals.

javier.description: Security / SRE · threat modelling, OWASP, secrets.

noah.description: Technical Writer · READMEs, API refs, runbooks.

lena.description: Senior Frontend Engineer · React, TypeScript, design systems.

diego.description: Senior Backend Engineer · Node, Postgres, APIs.

aiko.description: Mobile Engineer · iOS, Android, React Native.

priya.description: QA Engineer · test strategy, Playwright, accessibility.

maya.description: Product Designer · UX, IA, design tokens.

sections.builtinSub_one: {{count}} seed agent · always available in chat
sections.builtinSub_other: {{count}} seed agents · always available in chat
sections.yourSubEmpty: Build a custom persona for one-off tasks

deleteConfirm.title: Delete {{name}}?
deleteConfirm.usedOn_one: This agent is used on {{count}} team. Deleting will unlink it — the team's role keeps its inline config.
deleteConfirm.usedOn_other: This agent is used on {{count}} teams. Deleting will unlink it — the team's role keeps its inline config.
deleteConfirm.unused: This agent is not used on any team. Chat threads will be removed.

dialog.descCreate: Define a reusable agent. Chat history attaches to it across projects.
dialog.descEdit: Changes take effect immediately. Existing threads keep history.
dialog.name: Name
dialog.systemPromptPlaceholder: You are an opinionated TypeScript architect. You evaluate trade-offs honestly and prefer minimal abstractions…

errors.promptTooLong: System prompt is too long ({{len}}/{{max}} chars).

agents.emptyDescription: Create your first agent to start building teams.

projectMenu.deleteConfirmBody: {{name}} will be permanently removed — runs, plans, and team configuration will be lost.

newProject.title: New Project
newProject.description: Create a project to start configuring a team and plan.

fields.name: Name
fields.goalPlaceholder: What should the agents accomplish? Be specific about the deliverable, the verification command, and any constraints.
fields.goalPlaceholderTemplate: Describe what you want to build. The {{template}} template's suggested goals will guide you.

repoStatus.notGitRepo: Folder exists but isn't a git repo — WISP will initialise it for you.
repoStatus.missing: Folder doesn't exist yet — WISP will create and initialise it automatically when you continue.

teamBuilder.title: Team Builder
teamBuilder.selectProject: Select a project to configure its team.
teamBuilder.subtitle: Configure the agents for {{name}}.
teamBuilder.unsavedHint: Default starter team — not yet saved to this project.

addAgent.title: Add a built-in agent
addAgent.description: Pick one of the ready-made agents (the same ones you can chat with) to add as a team role. You can still tweak its model, tools and prompt afterwards.

saveAsTemplateDialog.title: Save team as template
saveAsTemplateDialog.description: Stores this team configuration to disk under your data dir. You can pick it from the New Project dialog later.
saveAsTemplateDialog.name: Name (≥2 chars)
saveAsTemplateDialog.descriptionPlaceholder: What this team is good for…

generateTitle.noTeamUnsaved: Configure roles and save the team first
generateTitle.saveFirst: Save the team first to generate a plan
generateTitle.unsaved: Unsaved changes: save first to generate a plan
generateTitle.noGoal: Set a goal on the project first
generateTitle.ready: Generate a plan from the saved team and goal

toasts.briefNotReadyHint: Plan generation needs a finalised brief. Opening the Brief tab — answer a few questions or use your goal as the brief, then generate.

preview.title: What the agent sees
preview.description: The system prompt is fixed per role. The task prompt below is what the walker composes and feeds to claude -p — pick a role, edit the sample inputs, and inspect the result.

fields.toolsHint: Restrict what this agent can call. Leave empty to grant Claude Code defaults.

planEditor.title: Plan Editor
planEditor.selectProject: Select a project to view its plan.
planEditor.noPlanHint: Generate a plan from this project's team and goal.

node.noDeps: No other nodes to depend on.
node.instruction1: Click a node in the graph to edit it here.
node.instruction2: Drag nodes to rearrange the layout.

lockDialog.title: Lock & Run this plan?
lockDialog.titleHasUnsaved: Save unsaved changes and Lock & Run?
lockDialog.description: Locking prevents further edits and immediately starts a run with the current plan. The architect, developer, and QA agents will spawn against your repo. You can pause or cancel from the run view.
lockDialog.runDescription: Starts a run from the locked plan immediately. Tasks dispatch into git worktrees and begin executing. You can pause or cancel from the run view.

regenDialog.title: Regenerate plan?
regenDialog.description: This replaces the current draft with a freshly generated plan from the team and goal.

canvas.empty: No tasks in this plan. Regenerate to rebuild.

repoInit.title: This project's directory isn't a git repository yet.
repoInit.body: Agents work in isolated git worktrees. Initialize a fresh git repo and an initial commit, then we'll retry Lock & Run automatically. Directory:

errors.planInvalidRoles: The planner used roles that are not in your team: {{roles}}. Please regenerate the plan or adjust your team.

runView.title: Run View
runView.loadFailedDesc: The server returned an error or the run id is invalid.
runView.notFoundDesc: This run does not exist or has been deleted.
runView.loadFailedBody: The dashboard server returned an error while fetching this run. The run may still be running on the backend — try retrying.

autopilot.label: Autopilot
autopilot.tooltip: Auto-resumes the run when it's paused by rate-limit or server restart. User-pause + structural failures are NOT auto-resumed. Stops at the Budget Min/Tokens cap.

cancelDialog.title: Cancel run?
cancelDialog.description: This stops the walker and marks the run as cancelled. In-flight subprocesses may take a moment to terminate.

toasts.budgetWarningDesc: Approaching the configured budget for this run.

tooltip.wire-up: Auto-inserted: reconciles the parallel work branches so the project builds.
tooltip.runtime-verifier: Auto-inserted: boots the app and verifies it actually runs.
tooltip.lead: Auto-inserted: the team lead reviews the result at the end of the plan.
tooltip.default: Added by the system — not part of the team you picked.

firstRunModal.title: WISP uses your Claude subscription
firstRunModal.description: This tool spawns claude -p subprocesses inheriting your ~/.claude/ credentials. Long-running runs consume your subscription quota. Review the Anthropic ToS — running intensive headless workflows is your responsibility.
firstRunModal.footnote: You will see this once. Acknowledgment is stored in browser localStorage.

testPrompt.title: Test prompt — {{role}}
testPrompt.description: Sends the system prompt + a sample goal to claude -p with --max-turns 2. Subscription quota applies.
testPrompt.loginHint: Hint: run `claude login` on the server host first.

nextSteps.title: Getting started
nextSteps.subtitle: A few steps to your first run.
nextSteps.briefDesc: First step — needed before planning. Answer Sarah's questions or take your goal as the brief.
nextSteps.teamDesc: Pick the agents that will build your project.
nextSteps.planDesc: Create the task plan, then lock & run.

templatePicker.noTemplateHint: Start with the default 3-role team and write your own goal.

web-app.name: Web app (React + Vite)
web-app.description: Builds a browser web app with React, Vite and Tailwind. Pipeline: Architect plans the work, a UI developer builds the screens, a test developer writes unit and browser tests, and QA runs install, type-check, test and build.
web-app.exampleGoal: Build a single-page React + Vite + Tailwind tip calculator: enter a bill amount and tip percent, show per-person totals, with Vitest tests for the math and a Playwright smoke test asserting the page renders.

fullstack-web.name: Full-stack web app
fullstack-web.description: Builds one web feature end to end: a backend API plus the React UI that uses it. Pipeline: Architect → backend dev → frontend dev → tests → QA.
fullstack-web.exampleGoal: Build a notes feature: an Express API with GET/POST/DELETE /api/notes backed by an in-memory store, plus a React page that lists, adds, and deletes notes, with Vitest API tests and a Playwright e2e covering the add-then-delete round-trip.

mobile-app.name: Mobile app (React Native / Expo)
mobile-app.description: Builds a cross-platform mobile app with React Native and Expo. Pipeline: Architect → mobile dev (screens + navigation) → test dev (component tests) → QA (typecheck / lint / test).
mobile-app.exampleGoal: Build an Expo Router app with a tab navigator: a Home screen listing tasks from local state and a Settings screen with a dark-mode toggle, plus component tests for both.

desktop-app.name: Desktop app (Tauri)
desktop-app.description: Builds a cross-platform desktop app with Tauri (Rust shell + web UI). Pipeline: Architect → UI dev → packaging engineer → tests → QA, ending in an installable build artifact.
desktop-app.exampleGoal: Build a cross-platform Tauri notes app: a React UI with a sidebar list and editor, persisting notes to the local filesystem via a Rust #[tauri::command], with a unique bundle identifier and Windows msi output.

cli-tool.name: CLI tool
cli-tool.description: Builds a command-line tool in Node/TypeScript with argument parsing and tests. Pipeline: Architect plans the commands and flags, a core dev implements the CLI, a test dev writes Vitest tests, then QA runs typecheck, tests, and build.
cli-tool.exampleGoal: Build a `greet` CLI with a `--name` flag (default 'world') and a `--shout` boolean that uppercases the output, with Vitest tests for defaults, both flags, and an unknown-flag error.

ts-library.name: TypeScript library
ts-library.description: Architect → 2 devs → QA. Optimised for small TypeScript libraries with build / test / lint gates.
ts-library.exampleGoal: Add a hello(name) function returning 'Hello <name>!' to src/hello.ts plus a Vitest test covering happy path and empty-string edge case.

python-backend.name: Python backend
python-backend.description: Architect → API dev → test dev → QA. Targets small Python services with pytest gates.
python-backend.exampleGoal: Add a /health endpoint to src/app.py returning {status: 'ok'} with a pytest test using Flask's test client.

docs-site.name: Docs & guides
docs-site.description: Architect → tech writer → QA. Builds documentation only: READMEs, how-to guides, API references, and runbooks. No code, just clear writing checked for accuracy and working links.
docs-site.exampleGoal: Write a README.md for this repo covering what it does, install, quick start, configuration, and a troubleshooting section, with all commands verified against package.json.

refactor-squad.name: Refactor squad
refactor-squad.description: Architect (planner) → refactor-dev (opus, careful execution) → QA. Optimised for safe code transformations that must keep all existing tests green.
refactor-squad.exampleGoal: Extract the foo() function from src/big.ts into a new module src/foo.ts while keeping the public re-export so all existing tests pass.

data-pipeline.name: Data pipeline
data-pipeline.description: Architect → pipeline-dev → validator → QA. For ETL-style transforms with input/output schema validation.
data-pipeline.exampleGoal: Read input/users.csv with columns name,email,age and produce output/users.json with the same fields, normalising email to lowercase and rejecting rows where age is not a positive integer.

home.title: Mission Control
home.subtitle: Live overview of all agent runs across your projects.

live.title: Live now
live.empty: No active runs — kick one off from a project.

byProject.title: By project
byProject.subtitle: OK = all-time success rate · all runs
byProject.empty: No projects yet — create one from the sidebar.

recentRuns.title: Recent runs
recentRuns.subtitle: all projects · sortable
recentRuns.empty: No runs yet — create a project, generate a plan, lock & run.

greeting.idle: No live agents · ready when you are.

projectDetail.subtitle: Project overview, team, plan, and run history.

runStart.title: Start a new run?
runStart.description: The run will use the current project goal shown below and the locked plan.
runStart.iterationHint: Only want to change what's there? → Switch to the preview tab, add change-requests, and start an iteration instead.

runs.title: Run history
runs.empty: No runs yet — generate a plan and lock it to start the first one.

toasts.repoInitialized: Initialized the project's git repo — starting the run

repoEdit.hint: Point this at a local git repo, or an empty folder WISP can initialise. Changing it won't migrate existing worktrees or result branches.

goalEdit.placeholder: Describe what the agents should accomplish.
goalEdit.hint: Re-generate the plan after saving to use the new goal in future runs.

productionMode.title: Production mode
productionMode.description: Deliver a truly finished app in one goal: auto-merge into main + automatic hardening runs.
productionMode.autoMergeDescription: Fast-forwards the result branch into main after every successful run.
productionMode.selfHealingDescription: Scans docs/security-review.md + docs/qa-report.md for HIGH/CRITICAL and spawns a hardening run if any remain.
productionMode.maxIterationsDescription: Cap on consecutive self-healing runs (1–10). Prevents infinite loops.
productionMode.autopilotDefaultDescription: Auto-enables autopilot for every new run of this project — still overridable per run.
productionMode.budgetDescription: Stops autopilot when minutes or tokens exceed the cap (empty = unlimited).

projectState.title: Project state
projectState.description: What this project does today — extracted by the runtime-verifier after the last successful run. The iteration planner reads this to plan a delta.

orgChart.title: Team org-chart
orgChart.description: Roles, hand-offs and live activity for this project's team.

empty.title: No saved team yet
empty.description: The Team Builder may show a starter team, but it isn't saved to this project until you press Save. Save the team there to see the org-chart here.

agentOverride.title: Customise {{role}}
agentOverride.description: Project-specific overrides are appended to the base agent definition. Leave blank to fall back to the default.

preview.title: Preview
preview.description: Boots the project's dev server inside the harness and frames it here. Useful for an eye-test between iterations.
preview.empty: No preview running. Start the dev server to see the app here.

errors.no_dev_cmd: No web preview is available for this project yet. Run the team once so it builds the app — or set a dev command in the project settings.
errors.worktree_setup_failed: Could not prepare the preview (installing dependencies failed). Try again; if it keeps failing, check the repo path and that pnpm is installed.
errors.repo_not_initialized: This project's folder isn't a git repository yet. Open the project, initialize the repo, then try again.

toasts.iterationPreparingDescription: Generating a new plan based on your change-requests. This may take one to two minutes.

edit.promptPlaceholder: What should change about this element?

changes.title: Pending change requests
changes.pending: These will be addressed on the next Run Iteration.
changes.textPlaceholder: Describe a free-form change (no element pick needed)…

briefCard.title: Project brief
briefCard.descriptionReady: The brief is set. The agents plan the work from it.
briefCard.descriptionPending: First step: describe what should be built. Answer Sarah's questions — or take your goal as the brief in one click.
briefCard.finalizeHint: Lock the brief and write docs/PRD.md.
briefCard.finalizeEarlyHint: You can finalise early but the planner will have less context.
briefCard.useGoalAsBriefHint: Quickest path: take your goal as the brief — you can still refine it later.
briefCard.requiredHint: The brief is the first step and is needed before a plan can be generated. Either answer Sarah's questions — or take your goal as the brief in one click.
briefCard.explainer: What is the brief? Here you describe, in your own words, what should be built. The agents read it to plan the work and get started. The clearer the brief, the better the result.
briefCard.loadError: The brief couldn't be loaded. Please try again in a moment.

welcome.greeting: Hi, I'm Sarah. I'll help you turn your idea into a short brief the agents can build from.
welcome.prompt: Just start typing — or answer one of these questions:

import.title: Import an existing plan as the brief
import.description: Paste an existing markdown document or upload a file. It becomes docs/PRD.md and the brief is finalised right away — without Sarah's interview.
import.textareaPlaceholder:
# My project plan

Paste your markdown here …
import.previewEmpty: The preview appears once you paste some content.

toasts.finalizedWithWarning: Brief finalised, but PRD could not be written
toasts.importedWithWarning: Brief imported, but PRD could not be written

leadNotes.title: Team Lead — Theo
leadNotes.description: Theo synthesises project state, events, and handoffs into a routing decision. Manual ticks only in v2.0.
leadNotes.empty: No lead notes yet. Trigger a tick to get Theo's synthesis.

applyTemplate.title: Apply a template
applyTemplate.description: Replaces the current draft team with the template.
applyTemplate.loads: Loads the chosen template into the current TeamBuilder draft.
applyTemplate.willOverwrite: Your current draft will be overwritten.

teamJsonDialog.title: Team configuration (JSON)
teamJsonDialog.description: What gets sent to PUT /api/projects/:id/team. Useful for sharing a config or moving it between machines.

costPanel.title: Cost / quota estimate
costPanel.description: Subscription mode doesn't bill per-token; this is a relative-weight readout plus an empirical projection from this project's prior runs.
costPanel.noData: No prior successful runs in this project — projection unavailable until you ship at least one run.

chat.notSeeded: Team chat is not seeded yet.
chat.notSeededBody: The built-in dev team (Marcus + 9 specialists) is installed automatically on first server boot. Restart the dashboard server, then return here.

thread.deleteConfirm: Delete this conversation? This cannot be undone.

composer.placeholder: Message Marcus + team — Enter to send, Shift+Enter for a new line. Type @ to mention a teammate.

participants.title: People
participants.permanent: Marcus stays in every conversation. Add specialists to bring them into the chat — or @mention them inline.

addMember.title: Add to conversation
addMember.emptyCustom: No custom agents yet. Create one in Agents settings.

greeter.title: Brainstorm with the team
greeter.body: Marcus (Project Manager) chairs every conversation. He can pull specialists in, then create + start a project once you've agreed on direction.
greeter.starterGreeting: Hi! Tell me what you're thinking about — a feature idea, a bug to chase, or a fresh project. I'll loop in the specialists you need.
prompts: What is WISP and how do I use it?
Plan a feature with the team

action.projectTeam: Team of {{count}}. Ready to plan & run.
action.projectCreatedNoLink: Project created — refresh to open it.
action.noPlan: Cannot start run yet — no plan. Open the project to generate one.

agentChat.noAgentsBody: Create your first agent to start chatting.
agentChat.noThreads: no threads — type below to start one.
agentChat.startConversation: Type a message below to start the conversation.

avatarPicker.title: Choose a profile picture
avatarPicker.footer: Pick a picture or use an initials fallback. The same picture can be reused across agents.

buildApp.title: Build App
buildApp.description: Package the current successful build into a native installer.
buildApp.disabledHint: Set packageTarget to a non-web value to enable native packaging.

disabled.noSuccess: Needs at least one successful run.

errors.tauri_cli_missing: Tauri CLI not found. Install it with: pnpm add -g @tauri-apps/cli
errors.rust_toolchain_missing: Rust toolchain not found. Install Rust: https://rustup.rs
errors.web_build_failed: Web build (pnpm build) failed. Check the project's build script.
errors.tauri_build_failed: Tauri build failed. Inspect docs/build-manifest.json + the run log.
errors.artifact_not_found: Tauri build finished but no installer was produced.
errors.unsupported_target: This packageTarget is not implemented yet.

toasts.success: Build complete — installer ready to download.

target.title: Build target
target.description: Choose how the project should be shipped. Currently only tauri-exe is implemented.

tooltips.sendMessage: Send message (Enter — Shift+Enter for a new line)
tooltips.addMember: Add a specialist to this thread
tooltips.autopilotEnable: Enable autopilot — resume paused runs automatically
tooltips.continueFailedRun: Continue this run — finished steps are kept, only the failed step is re-attempted
tooltips.cancelRun: Cancel and clean up the run

tooltips.regeneratePlan: Re-generate the plan from current team + goal
tooltips.saveAsTemplate: Save current team as a reusable template
tooltips.resetBundle: Reset cached bundle (next call will re-warm)

data.title: Manage data
data.explanation: Delete collected data by category. This is permanent — no undo.

confirm.title: Really clear all {{category}}?
confirm.body: All {{count}} entries in '{{category}}' will be permanently removed.

notes.runs: Run history is read-only — runs are derived from projects. Delete projects to remove their runs.

focus.title: Focusboard
focus.noProjectsHint: Create a project from the sidebar, then you can launch it here.

notFound.title: This view does not exist.
notFound.body: The URL doesn't match any route. Likely a stale link or a typo.

errorBoundary.title: Something went wrong.
errorBoundary.body: This view threw an unexpected error. Try again — if it keeps happening, please report it as an issue.

auth.bannerFallback: The claude CLI is not authenticated. Run "claude login", then restart the dashboard.
